news.title: MHADA reviews progress of housing projects in Kalyan taluka
news.para: MHADA vice-president and CEO Sanjeev Jaiswal reviewed the progress of housing projects being developed by the Konkan Housing and Area Development Board at Khoni, Shirdhon, Gotheghar and Bhandarli in Thane district. The projects include large-scale housing for economically weaker and low-income groups, along with shops and civic amenities.

news.title: UP-RERA asks banks to enforce revised norms for real estate project accounts
news.para: UP-RERA has asked banks and financial institutions to follow revised operational guidelines for RERA-registered project accounts, including a mandatory three-account system, restrictions on liens, tighter withdrawal norms and quarterly disclosures of project finance. The authority said the guidelines are aimed at preventing diversion of homebuyer funds and improving monitoring of real estate projects.

news.title: MHADA starts allotment of 827 rehab units in Worli BDD Chawl redevelopment project
news.para: MHADA has started issuing allotment letters and possession of rehabilitation tenements in the first rehab building of the Worli BDD Chawl redevelopment project. On the first day, 41 eligible residents were issued allotment letters and 36 residents who completed documentation were given possession of their units.

news.title: Telangana CM directs officials to formulate GCC policy
news.para: Telangana Chief Minister A. Revanth Reddy on Wednesday instructed officials to formulate a comprehensive Global Capability Centres (GCC) policy to encourage and incentivise companies to establish their bases beyond the Hyderabad Core Urban Regional Economy (CURE) area.

news.title: Sundaram Alternates closes real estate credit fund at ₹2,500 crore
news.para: Sundaram Alternates has closed its fifth real estate credit fund at ₹2,500 crore, exceeding its initial target corpus of ₹1,500 crore. The fund, launched in October 2025, has already committed over 90% of the capital raised and will invest in senior-secured debt opportunities in brownfield residential projects.

news.title: White Flower Developers to acquire 6,000 sq meters land for ₹65 crore in Goa
news.para: White Flower Developers is planning to enter the Goa real estate market through the acquisition of three villa projects in Assagao, Tivim and Siolim, with a combined estimated project value of about ₹130 crore. The planned portfolio includes 13 villas across Assagao and Tivim and a heritage villa property in Siolim.

news.title: WeWork India posts net profit of ₹52.89 crore in FY26
news.para: WeWork India reported a net consolidated profit of ₹52.89 crore for FY26, with total income reaching ₹2,490.42 crore. The company expanded its footprint to 8.6 million sq ft across 76 centers, achieving 86.9% portfolio occupancy. Notably, WeWork India ended the year with a net debt-negative position of –₹11.7 crore.

news.title: JSW Cement's net loss widens to ₹798.78 crore in FY26
news.para: JSW Cement faced a significant net consolidated loss of ₹798.78 crore in the financial year 2025-26. However, the company's total income saw a healthy growth of 12.68 percent. The fourth quarter of FY26 showed a profit of ₹361.65 crore. The company plans a ₹430 crore investment for new capacity.

news.title: Karyan Group launches Trevana Residences' on NH-24, Ghaziabad; targets ₹1,500 crore revenue amid rising NCR housing demand
news.para: Karyan Group is launching Trevana Residences, a luxury housing project on NH-24 in Ghaziabad. The project involves a ₹900 crore investment and aims for ₹1,500 crore revenue. It will feature 3 and 4 BHK homes with modern amenities. The development is scheduled for completion by 2030. Ghaziabad's NH-24 corridor is seeing significant growth and investor interest.

news.title: India’s real estate sector may need ₹50 lakh crore capital by 2036: Report
news.para: India’s real estate sector may require nearly ₹50 lakh crore of capital over the next decade to support growth, according to an Anarock Capital report. While funding channels have diversified through banks, AIFs, REITs, private credit and government-backed vehicles, affordable housing remains underfunded, with over 4.5 lakh stalled affordable and mid-income homes needing about ₹55,000 crore of support.

news.title: KIADB acquires 838 acres for phase-II expansion in Chamarajanagar
news.para: Karnataka Industrial Area Development Board has acquired 838 acres for the expansion of the Kellamballi-Badanaguppe Industrial Area in Chamarajanagar district. Compensation is being distributed to landowners. Development work has begun to create industrial plots. This expansion aims to attract more investors and generate jobs in the region.

news.title: E-khata, Kaveri 2 changes disrupt property transactions in Belagavi
news.para: Property deals face major hurdles due to new e-Khata and Kaveri 2 systems. Belagavi MP Jagadish Shettar highlighted issues with perpetual lease properties and company ownership.  Transactions are stalled, impacting revenue and causing financial stress.  Shettar calls for urgent government intervention to fix the system and restore normalcy.

news.title: Uttarakhand to get first construction waste processing plant in Dehradun
news.para: Uttarakhand's first construction and demolition waste processing plant is set to be established in Dehradun's Mothrowala. With land secured and environmental clearance granted, the facility will convert debris into reusable materials like bricks and blocks. This initiative aims to combat dust pollution and address the issue of indiscriminate waste dumping across the city.

news.title: Hotels, guesthouses get industry status in Ladakh
news.para: Ladakh's lieutenant governor has granted 'industry' status to registered hotels and guesthouses, effective June 1. This move aims to bolster the hospitality sector by allowing 1,257 establishments to access industrial rates for electricity and water. The reform is expected to provide incentives, concessions, and infrastructure support, making businesses more sustainable and enhancing Ladakh's appeal as a tourism destination.

news.title: Centre ready in principle for 50:50 funding of Hyderabad metro phase II: Union coal minister
news.para: The Union Coal Minister announced the Centre's readiness to co-fund the Hyderabad Metro's second phase on a 50:50 basis. Union Minister Manohar Lal assured positive consideration pending the Detailed Project Report. Additionally, the Centre has sanctioned 39 sewage treatment plants for the Musi River at Rs 3,975 crore under the AMRUT scheme.

news.title: IRDAI may ease norms for insurance companies to invest more in REITs, InvITs
news.para: Insurers may soon see relaxed investment rules for real estate and infrastructure trusts. The regulator is considering a combined 6% exposure limit, up from the current 3% for each. Strong investor interest in recent infrastructure offerings, like the Raajmarg InvIT, has fueled demands for greater insurer participation in these long-duration yield assets.

news.title: Adani, Reliance, Lodha, JSW bid for 206-acre Mumbai redevelopment projects
news.para: Leading real estate firms are vying to redevelop three large housing colonies in Mumbai. These projects span prime areas like Worli, Bandra Reclamation, and Andheri West. The Maharashtra Housing and Area Development Authority is overseeing the process. This initiative aims to upgrade living standards for residents in older colonies. It promises modern housing and improved urban infrastructure for thousands.

news.title: Embassy Developments posts net loss of ₹872.47 crore in FY26
news.para: Embassy Developments faced a substantial net consolidated loss of ₹872.47 crore in the financial year 2025-26. This marks a sharp turnaround from the profit recorded in the previous fiscal year. The company's total income also saw a considerable decline. In the fourth quarter of FY26, the financial performance continued to be challenging.

news.title: Indiqube Spaces posts net loss of ₹106.34 crore in FY26
news.para: Indiqube Spaces reported a loss of ₹106.34 crore for FY26, despite total income reaching ₹1,527.51 crore. The co-founder highlighted significant expansion, with a footprint across 17 cities and 9.66 million sq ft of office space, adding 28,000 seats and maintaining 88% occupancy. The company also saw its debt-to-equity ratio improve substantially.

news.title: Arvind SmartSpaces' net profit dips 13.22% to ₹103.41 crore in FY26
news.para: Arvind SmartSpaces reported a 13.22% decline in net profit for FY26, with profit after tax at ₹103.41 crore. Total income also saw a 20.49% dip to ₹584.47 crore. However, Q4 FY26 profit after tax surged 102.94% to ₹44.16 crore. The company also plans to raise ₹300 crore via debt securities and has formed a new platform with HDFC Capital.
